description_long: This object is a military-style shoulder-fired rifle, specifically resembling a World War II-era bazooka or anti-tank rocket launcher. It features a long cylindrical barrel with a flared muzzle at the front and a rectangular blast shield near the rear, which is designed to protect the operator from backblast. The body is primarily constructed from metal, with visible welds and rivets, and is finished in an olive drab color typical of military equipment. There are simple iron sights mounted on the top, and a wire shoulder rest or support at the rear. The trigger mechanism and grip are located beneath the barrel, with a prominent rectangular housing. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on function over aesthetics.
description: A military-style, olive drab shoulder-fired rocket launcher with a long cylindrical barrel, blast shield, and simple iron sights. The design is rugged and utilitarian, featuring a wire shoulder rest and a rectangular trigger housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle opening and the protective blast shield behind it.
description_view_1: Side profile displaying the full length of the barrel, the rectangular blast shield, and the trigger housing beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the wire shoulder rest and the back of the blast shield.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the barrel, blast shield, and trigger housing from the other side.
